Ingest_LDD.name: COTS Camera
Ingest_LDD.comment: This Discipline LDD is intended for metadata specific to Commercial Off The Shelf (COTS) cameras. It will often contain EXIF-type keywords specific to a given camera model. The intent is to hold metadata that does not fit elsewhere. For example, if the IMG LDD has a place to store the metadata, it should generally be used in preference to this LDD.

DD_Attribute.name: camera_type
DD_Attribute.definition: The general type of camera. Corresponds to which type_Metadata class is used in this label. Type=None should not generally be used but is available if none of the type_Metadata classes apply.

DD_Attribute.name: camera_name
DD_Attribute.definition: The name including model number of the camera. This should be sufficiently precise that someone could go purchase a similar camera (assuming they're still being sold).

DD_Attribute.name: pitch
DD_Attribute.definition: The pitch angle of the camera body, generally perpendicular to the lens axis.

DD_Attribute.name: pitch_status
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates the trustworthiness of the pitch measurement. Possible values include "Fine", "Coarse", "Unusable". Note that unusable values may be simply omitted.

DD_Attribute.name: roll
DD_Attribute.definition: The roll angle of the camera body, generally about the lens axis.

DD_Attribute.name: roll_status
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates the trustworthiness of the roll measurement. Possible values include "Fine", "Coarse", "Unusable". Note that unusable values may be simply omitted.

DD_Attribute.name: yaw
DD_Attribute.definition: The yaw angle of the camera body, generally about the vertical axis at the camera location.

DD_Attribute.name: yaw_status
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates the trustworthiness of the yaw measurement. Possible values include "Fine", "Coarse", "Unusable". Note that unusable values may be simply omitted.

DD_Attribute.name: af_aperture
DD_Attribute.definition: The size of the lens opening, or aperture value, which controls the amount of light entering the camera during autofocusing.

DD_Attribute.name: af_area_mode
DD_Attribute.definition: Refers to the autofocus mode selected for focusing. Includes Single Point, Dynamic Area, and/or Auto Area AF. Mode availability depends on the capabilities of a specific camera model.

DD_Attribute.name: af_c_priority_selection
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates whether the camera will prioritize achieving focus or shot capture while in continuous autofocus mode and if the subject cannot be quickly locked on. Focus Priority will only capture a photograph once the subject has been properly focused. Release Priority will take a shot regardless of whether or not focus has been achieved.

DD_Attribute.name: af_detection_method
DD_Attribute.definition: Refers to the method the autofocus system uses to detect focus. This can include methods such as Contrast Detection or Phase Detection, depending on the capabilities of the camera model.

DD_Attribute.name: af_fine_tune
DD_Attribute.definition: Details, typically expressed in micro-adjustments (e.g., +5 or -10), on any calibration made to the autofocus system to improve accuracy.

DD_Attribute.name: af_info_2_version
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates the version number of the camera's autofocus information.

DD_Attribute.name: af_point_brightness
DD_Attribute.definition: Refers to the intensity of the indicator that shows which focus point(s) are selected.

DD_Attribute.name: af_points_in_focus
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates how many of the selected autofocus points achieved focus on the subject.

DD_Attribute.name: af_points_used
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates how many autofocus points where actively engaged during the shot.

DD_Attribute.name: af_s_priority_selection
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates whether the camera prioritizes on achieving a sharp focus before allowing the shutter to release in single autofocus mode. Focus Priority will only release the shutter once it has achieved focus on the subject. Release Priority will allow the shutter to be released even if focus has not been achieved.

DD_Attribute.name: block_shot_af_response
DD_Attribute.definition: Describes the camera's autofocus behaviour when shooting in burst mode. This indicates whether focus is locked for the entire burst or adjusted between shots.

DD_Attribute.name: focus_point_schema
DD_Attribute.definition: Describes the arragements or pattern of selected autofocus points.

DD_Attribute.name: focus_point_wrap
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates whether focus points wrap around when reaching the edge of the selection grid. Typically On or Off.

DD_Attribute.name: number_of_focus_points
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates how many autofocus points are available to the camera.

DD_Attribute.name: primary_af_point
DD_Attribute.definition: Specifies which autofocus point was primary when autofocusing. Typically expressed as an X, Y coordinate or numerical identifier of the autofocus point in use.

DD_Attribute.name: exit_pupil_position
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates the location of the exit pupil relative to the camera body or lens mount. This is typically measured in millimeters, affecting how light enters the camera.

DD_Attribute.name: flash_info_version
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates the version of the flash-related metadata recorded.

DD_Attribute.name: raw_image_center_line
DD_Attribute.definition: Specifies the coordinates of the center of the raw image as captired by the sensor

DD_Attribute.name: raw_image_center_sample
DD_Attribute.definition: TODO

DD_Attribute.name: shutter_count
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates the number of times the camera's shutter has been actuated since it's first use.

DD_Attribute.name: brightness
DD_Attribute.definition: Measures the overall lightness or darkness of the image as captured

DD_Attribute.name: clarity
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates the setting adjustment of the midtone contrast of an image. This adjustment affects the perceived sharpness and detail, specially the midtones, enhancing the overall texture without significantly altering highlights and shadows.

DD_Attribute.name: color_balance_version
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates the version of the color balance adjustments or algorithms applied to the image.

DD_Attribute.name: contrast
DD_Attribute.definition: Reflects the difference in brightness between the darkest and lightest parts of the image.

DD_Attribute.name: custom_settings_bank
DD_Attribute.definition: Numeric identifier representing the custom settings bank in use (e.g. Bank 1, Bank 2). The Custom Settings Bank allows users to store and select customized camera settings for specific shooting scenarios.

DD_Attribute.name: flash_mode
DD_Attribute.definition: Specifies the specific settings for the flash during the exposure, such as Normal or Red-Eye Reduction.

DD_Attribute.name: flicker_reduction
DD_Attribute.definition: Refers to a feature that helps reduce the effects of flickering light sources, such as flourescent or LED lights, which can cause banding or unwanted color shifts in images. Flicker reduction aims to synchronize the camera's shutter speed with the frequency of flickering lights, minimizing artifacts and ensuring more consistent exposure and color balance.

DD_Attribute.name: flicker_reduction_indicator
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates whether flicker reduction was used during the exposure, expressed as On or Off.

DD_Attribute.name: high_iso_noise_reduction
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates the method used in applying High ISO Noise Reduction.

DD_Attribute.name: noise_reduction
DD_Attribute.definition: Refers to whether the camera applied noise reduction algorithms to high-ISO images. Typically shown as On or Off.

DD_Attribute.name: hue
DD_Attribute.definition: A numeric scale or percentage of adjustment to the overall color tint of the image.

DD_Attribute.name: saturation
DD_Attribute.definition: A numeric scale or percentage of intensity of colors in the image.

DD_Attribute.name: sharpness
DD_Attribute.definition: Refers to the clarity and detail in the image expressed in a numerical scale (e.g., 0 to 100)

DD_Attribute.name: shooting_mode
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates the camera mode at the time of capture, affecting settings like focus and exposure. Includes Single Shot, Continuous, or Timer, depending on the capabilities of the camera.

DD_Attribute.name: shot_info_version
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates the version of detailed shooting parameters used for parsing specific camera settings.

DD_Attribute.name: vignette_control
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates whether the camera applied any correction for vignetting (brightness fall-off around corners).

DD_Attribute.name: auto_bracket_mode_m
DD_Attribute.definition: Refers to the specific mode used for exposure bracketing, often expressed in terms of settings. Could include modes like Exposure, Flash, or White Balance bracketing.

DD_Attribute.name: auto_bracket_order
DD_Attribute.definition: Denotes the order in which bracketed images are taken, such as whether they are taken from underexposed to overexposed, or vice versa.

DD_Attribute.name: center_weighted_area_size
DD_Attribute.definition: Defines the portion of the frame that the camera evaluates for exposure in Center-Weighted metering mode. This is typically expressed as a percentage.

DD_Attribute.name: cl_mode_shooting_speed
DD_Attribute.definition: Continuous Low mode shooting speed indicates the frame rate at which the camera captures image in continuous low mode. Usually expressed in frames per second (fps).

DD_Attribute.name: easy_exposure_compensation
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates whether the +/- button was needed to set exposure compensation with the main command dial

DD_Attribute.name: exposure_bracket_value
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates the amount by which exposure is varied in a series of bracketed shots. Represented in stops (e.g., +/- 1.0)

DD_Attribute.name: exposure_comp_step_size
DD_Attribute.definition: TODO

DD_Attribute.name: exposure_control_step_size
DD_Attribute.definition: TODO

DD_Attribute.name: exposure_delay_mode
DD_Attribute.definition: TODO

DD_Attribute.name: exposure_difference
DD_Attribute.definition: Records how much the exposure deviates from the camera's metered exposure. Often expressed in stops (e.g., +1.0 or -0.5)

DD_Attribute.name: iso_auto_high_limit
DD_Attribute.definition: Specifies the maximum ISO sensitivity that the camera will automatically adjust to when in Auto ISO mode.

DD_Attribute.name: iso_auto_shutter_time
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates the maximum shutter speed that the camera will use when set to Auto ISO mode. This defines the slowest shutter speed at which the camera will operate to avoid motion blur when increasing ISO sensitivity.

DD_Attribute.name: iso_step_size
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates the increments made in changes to ISO sensitivity.

DD_Attribute.name: max_continuous_release
DD_Attribute.definition: Specifies the maximum number of shots the camera can take in a continuous shooting burst.

DD_Attribute.name: multi_exposure_auto_gain
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates whether automatic gain control was applied to balance exposure across multiple exposures, typically On or Off.

DD_Attribute.name: multi_exposure_mode
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates which multiple exposure mode was used. Series takes a series of multiple exposures per shot resulting in multiple files. Single Photo will create only a single combined image.

DD_Attribute.name: multi_exposure_shots
DD_Attribute.definition: Denotes the number of exposures combined into a single image.

DD_Attribute.name: multi_exposure_version
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates the version of the multi-exposure settings used in the camera.

DD_Attribute.name: directory_number
DD_Attribute.definition: Designates the folder or directory within the memory card that contains the file

DD_Attribute.name: file_info_version
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates the version of the metadata structure of the file itself.

DD_Attribute.name: file_number
DD_Attribute.definition: Refers to the unique identifier assigned to the image file, typically a sequential number.

DD_Attribute.name: memory_card_number
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates the specific memory card in the camera where the image was saved, particaraly in cameras with more than one card slots.

DD_Attribute.name: effective_max_aperture
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates the largest aperture (in terms of light-gathering ability) used during the exposure, which may differ from the physical maximum aperture due to verious settings or conditions. This is expressed as an f-stop (e.g., f/4.0).

DD_Attribute.name: lens_data_version
DD_Attribute.definition: Signifies the version of the lens information records which may include updates specifications or characteristics

DD_Attribute.name: lens_f_stops
DD_Attribute.definition: Refers to the maximum and minimum aperture settings available on the lens. This is expressed in f-stop values (e.g., f/2.8 to f/22)

DD_Attribute.name: lens_id_number
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates a unique numeric identifier corresponding to the lens model used for the image capture.

DD_Attribute.name: lens_type
DD_Attribute.definition: Identifies the generation and techology of the attached lens, expressed as a numerical value which can be correlated to identifiers using specialized metadata types such as ExifTool.

DD_Attribute.name: max_aperture_at_max_focal
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates the widest aperture setting when the lens is at its maximum focal length, expressed as an f-stop (e.g., f/4.0)

DD_Attribute.name: max_aperture_at_min_focal
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates the widest aperture setting when the lens is at its minimum focal length, expressed as an f-stop (e.g., f/4.0)

DD_Attribute.name: max_focal_length
DD_Attribute.definition: Indicates the longest distance, in millimeters, that the lens can focus on.

DD_Attribute.name: min_focal_length
DD_Attribute.definition: Denotes the shortest distance, in millimeters, that the lens can focus on while still producing a sharp image.

DD_Attribute.name: mcu_version
DD_Attribute.definition: Represents the version of the microcontroller unit (MCU) firmware in the camera body that processes the images.

DD_Attribute.name: my_first_attribute
DD_Attribute.definition: My first attribute
